Title: Enzymes and cellular interplay required for flux of fixed nitrogen to ureides in bean nodules

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates xanthosine monophosphate (XMP) to xanthosine and phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot in tropical legumes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nodules, especially allantoate associated with inner cortex/vascular region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript induced in nodules versus roots (ratio 16.5)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in infected nodule cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ureide biosynthesis; xmpp nodules accumulate XMP and show reduced allantoate/allantoin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bean XMPP was biochemically validated as an efficient XMP phosphatase. 2. CRISPR xmpp nodules accumulated XMP, demonstrating in vivo XMP dephosphorylation by XMPP. 3. xmpp nodules had reduced ureides, showing XMPP contributes substantially but is not indispensable because a GMP-guanosine bypass exists.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression and enzyme assay; CRISPR knockout in hairy-root nodules; LC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Symbiotic nitrogen-fixing root nodules formed with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key entry step into purine catabolism for ureide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq meta-analysis: P. vulgaris PRJNA322335; G. max PRJNA79597 and PRJNA208048; L. japonicus PRJDB2819; M. truncatula PRJNA322355
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces ureides and causes XMP accumulation; blockage may increase flux to GMP/guanosine branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Kinetic parameters reported: Km 7.3 ± 2.4 µM, kcat 18.4 ± 1.7 s−1; expressed in infected cells while later steps occur in uninfected cells

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Slightly induced in nodules versus roots (ratio 2.8)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in infected nodule cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to ureide biosynthesis through the GMP-guanosine branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among three GSDA paralogs, GSDA.2 is the only one slightly induced in nodules. 2. GSDA mutant combinations showed reduced allantoate, demonstrating contribution of guanosine deamination to ureide synthesis. 3. This branch compensates partially when XMPP is blocked but is not the main route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout combinations; LC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq meta-analysis: P. vulgaris PRJNA322335; G. max PRJNA79597 and PRJNA208048; L. japonicus PRJDB2819; M. truncatula PRJNA322355
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Route is energetically more costly and may reflect unavoidable surplus GMP turnover
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GSDA.1 editing failed; conclusions rely on available gsda.2/gsda.3 and triple-mutant combinations. Promoters of GSDA.1, GSDA.2 and GSDA.3 were active in infected cells

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not induced in nodules versus roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in infected nodule cells; faint signal
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with other GSDA paralogs to GMP-guanosine branch supporting ureide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSDA.3 is one of three bean GSDA paralogs. 2. CRISPR gsda mutant combinations reduced allantoate, indicating the guanosine-to-xanthosine route contributes to ureide synthesis. 3. Its promoter is active in infected cells, consistent with branch operation there.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout combinations; LC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Low transcript abundance and faint promoter signal; individual contribution unresolved due paralogy

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes xanthosine to xanthine and ribose; also has weaker activity with inosine and guanosine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nodules, allantoate enriched toward inner cortex/vascular tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active exclusively in uninfected cells adjacent to infected cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Critical for ureide biosynthesis; nsh1 nodules accumulate xanthosine and have strongly reduced allantoate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSH1 physically interacts with NSH2 and NSH1/NSH2 complex has much higher xanthosine hydrolase activity than NSH1 alone. 2. CRISPR nsh1 nodules accumulated xanthosine and showed strong allantoate reduction, proving a critical role in ureide biosynthesis. 3. Promoter activity in uninfected cells indicates xanthosine is transferred from infected to uninfected cells before hydrolysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/co-purification; enzyme assays; CRISPR knockout; LC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for xanthosine utilization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes xanthosine/guanosine accumulation and strong decline in ureides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NSH1 mutation effectively removes both NSH1 and NSH2 complex activity because NSH2 activation depends on NSH1

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts in complex with NSH1 to strongly enhance xanthosine hydrolase activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nodules, allantoate enriched toward inner cortex/vascular tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active exclusively in uninfected cells adjacent to infected cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports efficient xanthosine hydrolysis in ureide biosynthesis through NSH1/NSH2 complex
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSH2 interacts with NSH1 in bean, contrary to an earlier tagged-protein report. 2. NSH2 alone lacked detectable xanthosine hydrolase activity, but the NSH1/NSH2 complex showed ~12-fold higher activity than NSH1 alone. 3. nsh2 single mutants showed no clear metabolite phenotype, suggesting NSH1 alone can suffice under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/co-purification; enzyme assays; CRISPR knockout; LC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supporting enhancer of key xanthosine hydrolysis step
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single loss showed little phenotype, so standalone value may be limited
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Guanosine inhibited NSH1/NSH2 activity; cell-type separation may avoid inhibition in infected cells

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes xanthine to urate; can also act on hypoxanthine but this route is not important here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nodules and export stream to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active predominantly in infected cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for ureide biosynthesis; xdh nodules accumulate xanthine and lack detectable allantoin and allantoate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR xdh nodules accumulated massive xanthine and completely lost detectable ureides. 2. Xanthine precipitates/fluorescent bodies formed in uninfected cells of xdh nodules. 3. Promoter analysis indicated XDH expression mainly in infected cells, placing a cell-type transition between xanthine formation in uninfected cells and urate formation in infected cells.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; LC-MS metabolite profiling; microscopy of xanthine precipitates; promoter-reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss abolishes ureide production and causes xanthine crystal accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Data argue against major inosine/hypoxanthine route because hypoxanthine did not accumulate in xdh nodules

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes urate to 5-hydroxyisourate, initiating conversion toward allantoin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Uninfected cells/peroxisomal zone and export path toward vascular tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in uninfected cells closely associated with infected cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports downstream ureide formation from urate in uninfected cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UOX promoter activity localizes to uninfected cells near infected cells. 2. This places urate oxidation after transfer of urate from infected to uninfected cells. 3. UOX expression supports the revised multi-cellular model of ureide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known background enzyme, but promoter localization was directly tested here and used to build the updated pathway model

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major long-distance nitrogen transport compound from nodules to shoot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cells around vascular bundles in inner cortex; allantoate concentrated toward vascular region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in cells surrounding vascular bundles in inner cortex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to conversion of allantoin into the major exported ureide allantoate near vasculature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALN promoter activity is localized mainly around vascular bundles in the inner cortex rather than the infection zone. 2. This indicates allantoin hydrolysis to allantoate occurs preferentially near the vasculature. 3. The expression pattern explains why allantoate is the dominant ureide product and helps create directed flux toward export tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen-fixing nodules with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for allantoate production site
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ALN is downstream of peroxisomal allantoin formation and localized in the ER
